calendar.matters:
STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-15-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

STATUS HEARING

On June 13, 2025, the court heard petitioner Jayson Alpon's petition to compel arbitration. The court's tentative stated as follows:

"Petitioner Jayson Alpon moves to compel arbitration against Blue Hill Specialty Insurance Company. Alpon, who was driving for Uber, was in a collision with a driver who did not stop. Petitioner made a claim against Blue Hill and the matter was set to go to arbitration pursuant to Insurance Code 11580.2(f). Blue Hill asked for a continuance because a passenger in Alpon's rideshare vehicle, Brian Limon Santiago, submitted a claim; Blue Hill now contends that Santiago is a necessary party and asks the court to order Santiago to arbitration as well.

"Santiago is not a party to this action and has not been served with process that would provide the court with jurisdiction to issue orders to him. The court orders the parties to arbitrate this dispute and to select an arbitrator within 90 days to schedule the arbitration. This gives Blue Hill time to secure Santiago's presence including through a petition to compel arbitration if needed.

"The court sets the matter for a status hearing on September 15, 2025 at 9 a.m. in Department 301. If the parties have not agreed on an arbitrator and commenced the arbitration by that date, each party is to submit three suggested arbitrators by supplemental briefing due five court days in advance of the continued hearing date."

No party has submitted supplemental briefing indicating that they require the court's assistance to select an arbitrator. The court therefore orders the matter off calendar.

Judge: Christine Van Aken: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CVA)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER JAYSON ALPON's Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner Jayson Alpon moves to compel arbitration against Blue Hill Specialty Insurance Company. Alpon, who was driving for Uber, was in a collision with a driver who did not stop. Petitioner made a claim against Blue Hill and the matter was set to go to arbitration pursuant to Insurance Code 11580.2(f). Blue Hill asked for a continuance because a passenger in Alpon's rideshare vehicle, Brian Limon Santiago, submitted a claim; Blue Hill now contends that Santiago is a necessary party and asks the court to order Santiago to arbitration as well.

Santiago is not a party to this action and has not been served with process that would provide the court with jurisdiction to issue orders to him. The court orders the parties to arbitrate this dispute and to select an arbitrator within 90 days to schedule the arbitration. This gives Blue Hill time to secure Santiago's presence including through a petition to compel arbitration if needed.

The court sets the matter for a status hearing on September 15, 2025 at 9 a.m. in Department 301. If the parties have not agreed on an arbitrator and commenced the arbitration by that date, each party is to submit three suggested arbitrators by supplemental briefing due five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Continued To Jun-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER JAYSON ALPON's Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration.

Continued to June 13, 2025, on the court's motion.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
